## Summary

I processed the **Fulu -- Honest Validator** specification from the Ethereum consensus specs and extracted **7 program graphs** corresponding to the functional units in the document.

### Functional Units Extracted

| ID | Name | Description | Nodes | Edges |
|:---|:-----|:------------|:-----:|:-----:|
| SG-001 | `get_validators_custody_requirement` | Computes minimum custody groups based on total effective balance of attached validators, clamped between `VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT` and `NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS` | 5 | 4 |
| SG-002 | `cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction` | Constructs cell-proof pairs from the blobs bundle by computing cells and slicing proofs for each blob | 7 | 7 |
| SG-003 | `get_data_column_sidecars` | Core function that assembles `DataColumnSidecar` objects by iterating over columns and collecting one cell/proof per blob per column | 11 | 12 |
| SG-004 | `get_data_column_sidecars_from_block` | Extracts commitments, computes block header and Merkle inclusion proof from a signed block, then delegates to `get_data_column_sidecars` | 6 | 4 |
| SG-005 | `get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar` | Reconstructs all sidecars from a single sidecar plus cells/proofs, reusing the existing sidecar's header and inclusion proof | 4 | 2 |
| SG-006 | `sidecar_publishing` | Publishes each sidecar to its deterministically computed subnet topic | 6 | 6 |
| SG-007 | `sidecar_retention` | Enforces the retention policy: sidecars MUST be held and served for `MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS` epochs, after which pruning MAY occur | 7 | 7 |

### Output Files

All `.mmd` files were written to:
```
outputs/graphs/W2B3_1770278556/fulu-validator/
```

Files:
- `SG-001_get_validators_custody_requirement.mmd`
- `SG-002_cells_and_kzg_proofs_construction.mmd`
- `SG-003_get_data_column_sidecars.mmd`
- `SG-004_get_data_column_sidecars_from_block.mmd`
- `SG-005_get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar.mmd`
- `SG-006_sidecar_publishing.mmd`
- `SG-007_sidecar_retention.mmd`

### Key Observations

1. **`get_data_column_sidecars` (SG-003) is the central function** -- both `get_data_column_sidecars_from_block` and `get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar` delegate to it after extracting/reusing the necessary context (header, commitments, inclusion proof).

2. **The custody requirement** (SG-001) uses a clamping pattern: `min(max(count, LOWER_BOUND), UPPER_BOUND)`, ensuring the return value is always within the valid range.

3. **The cells_and_kzg_proofs construction** (SG-002) relies on the modified `BlobsBundle` which includes cell KZG proofs instead of blob KZG proofs (a Fulu-specific change from EIP-7594).

4. **Sidecar retention** (SG-007) has an asymmetric obligation: holding is mandatory (MUST), pruning is optional (MAY), reflecting the protocol's data availability guarantees.

I processed the **Fulu -- Honest Validator** specification from the Ethereum consensus specs and extracted **7 program graphs** corresponding to the functional units in the document.

| SG-003 | `get_data_column_sidecars` | Core function that assembles `DataColumnSidecar` objects by iterating over columns and collecting one cell/proof per blob per column | 11 | 12 |
| SG-004 | `get_data_column_sidecars_from_block` | Extracts commitments, computes block header and Merkle inclusion proof from a signed block, then delegates to `get_data_column_sidecars` | 6 | 4 |
| SG-005 | `get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar` | Reconstructs all sidecars from a single sidecar plus cells/proofs, reusing the existing sidecar's header and inclusion proof | 4 | 2 |
| SG-006 | `sidecar_publishing` | Publishes each sidecar to its deterministically computed subnet topic | 6 | 6 |
| SG-007 | `sidecar_retention` | Enforces the retention policy: sidecars MUST be held and served for `MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS` epochs, after which pruning MAY occur | 7 | 7 |

### Key Observations

1. **`get_data_column_sidecars` (SG-003) is the central function** -- both `get_data_column_sidecars_from_block` and `get_data_column_sidecars_from_column_sidecar` delegate to it after extracting/reusing the necessary context (header, commitments, inclusion proof).

title: compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)

        INV: deg(Q) = deg(f) - deg(Z) = deg(f) - len(zs)

    q_ll --> q_lr : LR = KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n]

    q_rlp_init --> q_rlp_iter : h_k = coset_shift_for_cell(cell_indices[k])

title: construct_vanishing_polynomial (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)

    q_roots --> q_vanish : short_zero_poly = vanishing_polynomialcoeff(missing roots with bit-reversal)
    q_vanish --> q_init_ext : zero_poly_coeff = [0] * FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB

    q_extend --> q_assign : zero_poly_coeff[i * FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL] = coeff
    q_assign --> q_extend : next coefficient
    q_extend --> q_return : all coefficients placed
    q_return --> [*] : return zero_poly_coeff

        INV: At least one cell must be present (not all can be missing)
        INV: Vanishing polynomial evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points
        INV: Coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL extends polynomial to full domain

title: recover_polynomialcoeff (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start
    q_init --> q_domain : roots_ext = compute_roots_of_unity(FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
    q_domain --> q_flatten : build extended_evaluation_rbo with zeros for missing cells
    q_flatten --> q_permute : extended_evaluation = bit_reversal_permutation(extended_evaluation_rbo)
    q_permute --> q_missing : missing_cell_indices = cells not in cell_indices
    q_missing --> q_vanish : zero_poly_coeff = construct_vanishing_polynomial(missing_cell_indices)
    q_vanish --> q_zpeval : zero_poly_eval = fft_field(zero_poly_coeff, roots_ext)
    q_zpeval --> q_multiply : ez_eval = [a * b for a, b in zip(zero_poly_eval, extended_evaluation)]
    q_multiply --> q_ifft : ez_coeffs = fft_field(ez_eval, roots_ext, inv=True)
    q_ifft --> q_coset_ez : ez_over_coset = coset_fft_field(ez_coeffs, roots_ext)
    q_coset_ez --> q_coset_zp : zp_over_coset = coset_fft_field(zero_poly_coeff, roots_ext)
    q_coset_zp --> q_divide : reconstructed = [a / b for a, b in zip(ez_over_coset, zp_over_coset)]
    q_divide --> q_icoset : poly_coeff = coset_fft_field(reconstructed, roots_ext, inv=True)
    q_icoset --> [*] : return PolynomialCoeff(poly_coeff[:FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB])

    note right of q_vanish
        INV: E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over the FFT domain
        INV: Division by Z(x) is done over a coset to avoid division by zero
        INV: Recovered polynomial has degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB
        INV: At least 50% of cells must be available for successful recovery
    end note

title: _fft_field (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)

stateDiagram-v2

    q_split --> q_split_r : R = _fft_field(vals[odd], roots[even])

    q_return --> [*] : return r

    note right of q_outer
        INV: All xs values must be distinct (for invertibility)
        INV: Result polynomial has degree at most len(xs) - 1
        INV: evaluate(result, xs[i]) == ys[i] for all i
    end note

title: vanishing_polynomialcoeff (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start
    q_init --> q_iter : p = PolynomialCoeff([1])
    q_iter --> q_multiply : for x in xs
    q_multiply --> q_iter : p = multiply_polynomialcoeff(p, [-x, 1])
    q_iter --> q_return : all xs processed
    q_return --> [*] : return p

    note right of q_iter
        INV: Result polynomial evaluates to 0 at every point in xs
        INV: Result has degree exactly len(xs)
        INV: Leading coefficient is 1 (monic polynomial)
    end note

---
title: evaluate_polynomialcoeff (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start
    q_init --> q_iter : y = 0
    q_iter --> q_horner : for coef in polynomial_coeff reversed
    q_horner --> q_iter : y = y * z + coef
    q_iter --> q_return : all coefficients processed
    q_return --> [*] : return y

    note right of q_iter
        INV: Uses Horner's method for O(n) evaluation
        INV: Result equals sum of coeff[i] * z^i for all i
    end note

---
title: compute_kzg_proof_multi_impl (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start
    q_init --> q_eval : ys = [evaluate_polynomialcoeff(poly, z) for z in zs]
    q_eval --> q_vanish : denominator_poly = vanishing_polynomialcoeff(zs)
    q_vanish --> q_divide : quotient = divide_polynomialcoeff(poly, denominator_poly)
    q_divide --> q_commit : proof = g1_lincomb(KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL, quotient)
    q_commit --> [*] : return KZGProof(proof), ys

    note right of q_eval
        INV: Q(X) = f(X) / Z(X) where Z vanishes on all evaluation points zs
        INV: deg(Q) = deg(f) - deg(Z) = deg(f) - len(zs)
        INV: Proof is a commitment to the quotient polynomial
    end note

title: verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch_impl (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start

    q_assert --> q_validate : for each commitment_index assert < len(commitments)
    q_validate --> q_setup : num_cells = len(cell_indices), n = FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL

    q_powers --> q_ll : LL = g1_lincomb(proofs, r_powers)
    q_ll --> q_lr : LR = KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL[n]
    q_lr --> q_weights : weights = [0] * num_commitments
    q_weights --> q_weight_iter : weights[commitment_indices[k]] += r_powers[k]
    q_weight_iter --> q_rlc : all weights accumulated
    q_rlc --> q_interp_init : RLC = g1_lincomb(commitments, weights)
    q_interp_init --> q_interp_iter : sum_interp = PolynomialCoeff([0] * n)
    q_interp_iter --> q_interp_scale : interp_poly = interpolate(coset, cosets_evals[k])
    q_interp_scale --> q_interp_accum : scaled = multiply([r_powers[k]], interp_poly)
    q_interp_accum --> q_interp_iter : sum_interp = add(sum_interp, scaled)
    q_interp_iter --> q_rli : all interpolations done
    q_rli --> q_rlp_init : RLI = g1_lincomb(KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL[:n], sum_interp)
    q_rlp_init --> q_rlp_iter : h_k = coset_shift_for_cell(cell_indices[k])
    q_rlp_iter --> q_rlp : wrp = r_powers[k] * h_k.pow(n)
    q_rlp --> q_rl : RLP = g1_lincomb(proofs, weighted_r_powers)
    q_rl --> q_pairing : RL = RLC - RLI + RLP
    q_pairing --> [*] : pairing_check([[LL, LR], [RL, neg(G2)]])

    note right of q_challenge
        INV: Verification equation: e(LL, LR) == e(RL, [1])
        INV: Random challenge r binds all inputs via Fiat-Shamir
        INV: Commitments list must contain no duplicates
        INV: All commitment_indices must reference valid entries
        INV: Universal verification aggregates multiple cell proofs into single pairing check
    end note

---
title: coset_shift_for_cell (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2

    q_assert --> q_roots : roots = compute_roots_of_unity(FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB)

    q_permute --> [*] : return roots_brp[FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL * cell_index]

        INV: cell_index must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
        INV: Returns coset generator h for h*G where G is subgroup of order FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL

title: coset_for_cell (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start

    q_permute --> q_slice : start = FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL * cell_index
    q_slice --> [*] : return Coset(roots_brp[start : start + FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL])

        INV: cell_index must be in [0, CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
        INV: Returned Coset has exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements

    end note

title: construct_vanishing_polynomial (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)

stateDiagram-v2

    q_roots --> q_vanish : short_zero_poly = vanishing_polynomialcoeff(missing roots with bit-reversal)

    q_extend --> q_return : all coefficients placed

        INV: Vanishing polynomial evaluates to 0 at all missing cell evaluation points
        INV: Coefficient spacing by FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL extends polynomial to full domain
    end note

---
title: recover_polynomialcoeff (fulu-polynomial-commitments-sampling)
---
stateDiagram-v2
    direction TB

    [*] --> q_init : start
    q_init --> q_domain : roots_ext = compute_roots_of_unity(FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_EXT_BLOB)
    q_domain --> q_flatten : build extended_evaluation_rbo with zeros for missing cells
    q_flatten --> q_permute : extended_evaluation = bit_reversal_permutation(extended_evaluation_rbo)
    q_permute --> q_missing : missing_cell_indices = cells not in cell_indices
    q_missing --> q_vanish : zero_poly_coeff = construct_vanishing_polynomial(missing_cell_indices)
    q_vanish --> q_zpeval : zero_poly_eval = fft_field(zero_poly_coeff, roots_ext)
    q_zpeval --> q_multiply : ez_eval = [a * b for a, b in zip(zero_poly_eval, extended_evaluation)]
    q_multiply --> q_ifft : ez_coeffs = fft_field(ez_eval, roots_ext, inv=True)
    q_ifft --> q_coset_ez : ez_over_coset = coset_fft_field(ez_coeffs, roots_ext)
    q_coset_ez --> q_coset_zp : zp_over_coset = coset_fft_field(zero_poly_coeff, roots_ext)
    q_coset_zp --> q_divide : reconstructed = [a / b for a, b in zip(ez_over_coset, zp_over_coset)]
    q_divide --> q_icoset : poly_coeff = coset_fft_field(reconstructed, roots_ext, inv=True)
    q_icoset --> [*] : return PolynomialCoeff(poly_coeff[:FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB])

        INV: E(x) * Z(x) == P(x) * Z(x) over the FFT domain
        INV: Division by Z(x) is done over a coset to avoid division by zero
        INV: Recovered polynomial has degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB
        INV: At least 50% of cells must be available for successful recovery
    end note
